video.title: Giro d'Italia 2020 - Stage 16 Highlights | Cycling | Eurosport

transcript.clean_text: Stage 16 of the Giro d'Italia would be the second longest of the race and with almost 4,000 m of climbing one of the most difficult in terms of elevation. Several climbs to be taken on. Six categorized climbs in the foothills of the Julian Alps. The finish would take place in San Daniele del Friuli, the venue for the 1991 Italian National Championships. There's a big fight for the breakaway as expected with some huge mountain days in prospect. The GC guys behind allowed 28 men up the road and allowed them more than enough of a gap to take the stage. Giovanni Visconti would be fighting out for the blue mountains jersey. He'd lose just one point in that lead over the course of the stage. Behind there was an opportunity to shoot the breeze. A truce before the war to come to Madonna di Campiglio in 24 hours time. The war was on for the blue jersey. And I saw Ruben Guerreiro pulling some back. It was all about Giovanni Visconti on the day. 60 km to go and a first attack off the front onto the final few laps around the town of San Daniele del Friuli. Monte Ragogna to be taken on three times. Buaro and Tratnik the first riders going off the front. With a group of size as 28 there'd be some sort of argument and a lack of organization in the chase. Ben Swift tried to get things going. He take Tonelli with him alongside Ben O'Connor. Geoffrey Bouchard would be there and with 40 km to go penultimate ascent of the Monte Ragogna. Buaro dropped, Tratnik going away. And the Italian absorbed by those six chasers becoming seven. Question was could Tratnik hold on again? Looked as though he was going to do starting the last climb with 40 seconds of a gap but those 40 seconds were brilliantly closed by Ben O'Connor, 24 years of age from the west of Australia. The man from Perth would get there before the top, but it'd be the superstar from the Slovenian capital, Ljubljana, who would hold on and manage his effort well. Behind I think they were starting to understand that they were out of the game. Swift, Battaglin, Majka, and Bouchard. O'Connor would try and try, but he was not strong enough. Tratnik playing it tactically perfectly, and once he went around the Aussie, he'd open up a gap, and they wouldn't see each other again until after the finish line. 6 hours of really hard riding and a first ever grand tour stage win for Jan Tratnik, the Slovenian, winning just a few kilometers away from his home border. Ben O'Connor finishing in second place. NTT still looking for a new sponsor for next year. Jan Tratnik has the best day of his career. So, another Slovenian win. Bahrain-McLaren taking their first stage of this Giro d'Italia after 16 days. Well, they're just the seventh team to pick up a win. So many teams still haven't got anything out of this race. The pink jersey would get something. He'd certainly make a statement and pick up 2 seconds along the way. In the final kilometer, he could gap his general classification contenders. He now leads by 17 seconds. Wilco Kelderman losing a couple. 14 days it will be in the pink jersey for João Almeida, and lead extended again with a wonderful statement at the end of the race. Kelderman second, Hindley in third. Geoghegan Hart and Bilbao still in the top five, and 4:24 isn't the biggest gaps between first and 10th, given the fact we've got some huge mountains to come. Well, they begin with a five-star stage to Madonna di Campiglio. All of its history, good and bad at the Giro d'Italia. Up some big climbs on the way such as the Passo Durone. Before that, Monte Bondone as well. A long stage again over 200 km. Lots of meters of climbing and another day in the fight for pink.
